description: Seed fingerprints of code shapes historically associated with bugs. Each entry carries a canonical fingerprint + a prior bug rate (0..1) estimated from past healing data. The Bayesian audit check compares incoming files against these fingerprints and emits risk findings for matches above the configured threshold.

patterns.name: uncaught-json-parse-in-handler

fingerprint.skeleton: function IDENT ( IDENT ) { const IDENT = JSON . parse ( IDENT ) ; return IDENT . IDENT ; }

patterns.suggestion: Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch. Return null or a default on invalid input instead of letting a SyntaxError propagate into the caller.

patterns.name: mutable-sort-returned

fingerprint.skeleton: function IDENT ( IDENT ) { return IDENT . sort ( ) ; }

patterns.suggestion: The caller's array is mutated as a side effect. Copy before sort: `[...items].sort(...)`.

patterns.name: sql-via-template-literal

fingerprint.skeleton: function IDENT ( IDENT ) { return IDENT . query ( ` SELECT STAR FROM IDENT WHERE IDENT = $ { IDENT } ` ) ; }

patterns.suggestion: SQL via template literals is injection-prone. Use parameterized statements: `db.prepare('... WHERE id = ?').get(id)`.

patterns.name: exec-with-string-concat
patterns.suggestion: Shell exec with string concatenation enables command injection. Use execFile with an args array.

patterns.name: nullable-chain-without-guard

fingerprint.skeleton: const IDENT = IDENT ( IDENT ) ; return IDENT . IDENT . IDENT

patterns.suggestion: Chaining property access on a maybe-null callee crashes on the null path. Add a guard or use optional chaining.

patterns.name: check-then-set-shared-state

fingerprint.skeleton: if ( ! IDENT . IDENT ) { IDENT . IDENT = IDENT ; }

patterns.suggestion: Check-then-set on shared state is racy under concurrency. Use an atomic update, a mutex, or make the update idempotent.

patterns.name: division-by-length-unguarded

fingerprint.skeleton: IDENT . reduce ( ( IDENT , IDENT ) => IDENT + IDENT , 0 ) / IDENT . length

patterns.suggestion: Division by `.length` without a guard produces NaN on empty arrays. Use `arr.length > 0 ? sum / arr.length : 0`.
